formats.format: $timestamp$ HYPR eventType="$event_type$" user="$user$" device="$device$" isSuccessful=$is_successful$ authenticator="$authenticator$" message="$message$"

analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes HYPR authentication events, capturing user login attempts, registrations, verifications, enrollments, and revocations. It standardizes authentication logs into OCSF format with specific focus on identity and access management events, tracking success/failure states and authentication methods.

factors: Structured log format with clear delimiters

optimization_opportunities: Add input validation for required fields
Implement caching for repeated constant value lookups
Add field normalization for device names
Enhance error handling for malformed inputs
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: hypr_auth-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Authentication (3002)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:20:54.660922

-- Pre-define constant lookup tables for better performance
local ACTIVITY_MAPPINGS = {
    AUTHENTICATION = {id = 1, name = "Logon"},
    REGISTRATION = {id = 99, name = "Other"},
    VERIFICATION = {id = 99, name = "Other"}, 
    ENROLLMENT = {id = 99, name = "Other"},
    REVOCATION = {id = 99, name = "Other"}
}

local STATUS_MAPPINGS = {
    ["true"] = {id = 1, name = "Success"},
    ["false"] = {id = 2, name = "Failure"}
}

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize output structure with required OCSF fields
    local output = {
        -- Core OCSF classification
        class_uid = 3002,
        class_name = "Authentication",
        category_uid = 3, 
        category_name = "Identity & Access Management",
        type_uid = 300201,
        severity_id = 1,
        severity = "Informational",

        -- Initialize nested objects
        user = {},
        device = {}
    }

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    if record.timestamp then
        local ts = tonumber(record.timestamp)
        output.time = ts and ts * 1000 or os.time() * 1000
    else
        output.time = os.time() * 1000
    end

    -- Activity mapping with fallback
    if record.event_type then
        local activity = ACTIVITY_MAPPINGS[record.event_type:upper()]
        if activity then
            output.activity_id = activity.id
            output.activity_name = activity.name
        else
            output.activity_id = 99
            output.activity_name = "Other"
        end
    end

    -- Status mapping
    if record.is_successful then
        local status = STATUS_MAPPINGS[record.is_successful]
        if status then
            output.status_id = status.id
            output.status = status.name
        end
    end

    -- User information handling
    if record.user then
        output.user.email_addr = record.user
        output.user.name = record.user -- Copy email as username
    end

    -- Device information
    if record.device then
        output.device.name = record.device
    end

    -- Authentication protocol
    if record.authenticator then
        output.auth_protocol = record.authenticator
    end

    -- Message field
    if record.message then
        output.message = record.message
    end

    -- Final validation of required fields
    if not output.activity_id or not output.time then
        return nil, "Missing required fields"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error handler wrapper
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string.format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end
monitoring_recommendations: Monitor average processing time (<1ms per event)
Log invalid input patterns for analysis
